response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and systems engineer, but his profile does not match this role's core requirements. His C++ experience is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. He shows no SQL Server/Oracle depth (skills list generic SQL, MySQL, MongoDB) and no fixed-income, capital-markets, or investment-banking domain experience — RIDE Capital was a retail investment fintech using Node/TypeScript, not capital markets. The optional pluses (CI/CD, scripting, some Java) cannot compensate for missing every mandatory pillar.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems experience (DLang, async I/O, high-throughput services at Dunnhumby)
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tooling authorship
Broad polyglot background with proven ability to pick up new stacks quickly
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, and not recent (2014–2015)
No fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain exposure
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience, and no Windows-platform development focus
